title: LEFT JOIN with OR terms in WHERE clause causes assertion fault
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, age INTEGER, email TEXT UNIQUE);
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, amount REAL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age, email) VALUES ('Alice', 30, 'alice@example.com');
INSERT INTO users (name, age, email) VALUES ('Bob', NULL, 'bob@example.com');
SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE u.age > 25 OR o.amount IS NULL;
key_bug_features: LEFT JOIN with OR terms in WHERE clause
